review.revisit_condition: Revisit only if the license terms of a foundational input (the Quake II Rerelease game DLL or the Zaero source/assets) change.
review.notes: Zaero's original team released the Zaero game source and assets under the GNU GPL, and the Quake II Rerelease game DLL is GPL-2.0. All foundational inputs are GPL, so ZaeREo redistributes both code and media under the GPL with the original notices and credits preserved. Publishing a release remains a deliberate, human-approved step gated on engineering readiness, not on rights.

evidence.locator: checked-in effective Zaero asset identity inventory
evidence.supports: Identifies the runtime media carried forward by the port.

evidence.locator: Current policy and Records still required
evidence.supports: Records that the Zaero source and assets are GPL and describes the importer boundary used when rebuilding the pack from an existing installation.
Provides hashes for all supplied comparison trees and embeds the verified official Rerelease source identity.
Proves all 144 supplied Rerelease paths and blobs exactly match the selected immutable official GPL-2.0 commit/subtree.

evidence.locator: pinned vcpkg closure, SPDX schema, license hashes, and toolchain components
evidence.supports: Pins and validates the exact fmt/JsonCpp/build-helper license bytes and records the compiler/static-runtime scope.
Defines the component, mode, repository, CI, source-archive, and release-channel policy for redistributing the GPL work.
Defines the readiness profiles and the non-waivable release gates.
Contains the GPL-2.0 text under which the project and its GPL-released inputs are distributed.

evidence.locator: Zaero source additions; Zaero maps and media; dependency notices
evidence.supports: Records the component licenses and the required exact notice harvesting.

components.description: The Rerelease gameplay DLL tree with Zaero-derived behavior and integration changes intermingled with the upstream substrate.
components.holder: id Software (Rerelease substrate) and the original Zaero team (Team Evolve), both under the GPL, plus ZaeREo contributors
components.jurisdiction_scope: All public source and binary distribution channels; no jurisdiction-specific grant is recorded.
components.revisit_condition: Revisit if the license of the Rerelease substrate or the Zaero source changes.
components.notes: The Rerelease substrate is GPL-2.0 and the Zaero-derived additions are GPL, so the combined gameplay source and DLL are distributed under the GPL with corresponding source and notices.

components.description: Checked-in Rerelease reference material reconstructed from a supplied tree whose 144 paths and blobs exactly match a pinned official commit/subtree.
components.holder: id Software and other upstream contributors identified by the pinned official repository history
components.jurisdiction_scope: All public repository and source-archive channels.
components.revisit_condition: Revisit on a future upstream refresh.
components.notes: Reconstructed from a supplied tree that exactly matches a pinned official GPL-2.0 commit; distributed under the GPL with upstream notices retained.

components.description: Compatibility tests and source-derived audit records that encode detailed Zaero behavior or supplied-source facts.
components.jurisdiction_scope: All public repository, source-archive, CI, and release channels.
components.revisit_condition: Revisit if project authorship or licensing changes.
components.notes: Project-authored compatibility tests and audits, distributed as part of the corresponding source under the GPL.

components.description: Original Zaero maps, models, textures, images, sounds, sprites, cinematics, configuration, generated local derivatives such as glow maps, and other runtime media supplied by a user's installation.

external_inputs: Legitimate local Zaero installation: pak0.pak, pak1.pak, pak2.pak, and the nine documented loose runtime files

components.holder: The original Zaero team (Team Evolve), released under the GPL
components.jurisdiction_scope: All project-operated public hosting, CI, source, editor, and release channels.
components.revisit_condition: Revisit if the license of the Zaero assets changes.
components.notes: The Zaero assets are GPL. The port bundles the ported content into asset-full release packages with the original credits and notices preserved; the importer can also rebuild the pack from an existing installation.

components.description: Zaero episode configuration, mapdb fragment, and editor entity definitions.
components.holder: ZaeREo contributors, derived from GPL Zaero data
components.jurisdiction_scope: Public runtime packages and editor-gamepack channels.
components.notes: Configuration, mapdb fragment, and editor entity definitions, distributed under the GPL.

components.description: The standard-library acquisition, material-generation, PAK, and runtime-validation scripts proposed for a future tools-only artifact.
components.jurisdiction_scope: History-clean tools repository, CI, source-archive, and release-asset channels only.
components.notes: Project-authored automation, distributed under the GPL as part of the build/corresponding-source scripts.

components.description: Notices and generated metadata proposed for the future tools-only package.

external_inputs: Generated ACQUIRE_AND_BUILD.txt, BUILD-METADATA.json, MANIFEST.json, SBOM.spdx.json, VERSION.txt, and exact license copies

components.holder: Mixed license authors and ZaeREo contributors
components.revisit_condition: Revisit when the exact tools-only package is rendered.
components.notes: Notices and generated metadata for the tools-only package; the GPL text is redistributed verbatim.

components.description: fmt, JsonCpp, vcpkg build helpers, the MSVC compiler, and statically linked MSVC/runtime dependency bytes recorded for the Windows game DLL.

external_inputs: Pinned vcpkg-cmake and vcpkg-cmake-config build helpers
Microsoft Visual C++ 19.44.35222 and static runtime 14.44.35207

components.holder: fmt and JsonCpp copyright holders/contributors and Microsoft Corporation for the recorded compiler/runtime
components.jurisdiction_scope: Binary, source, SBOM, and notice bundles for all public DLL releases.
components.notes: fmt (MIT) and JsonCpp (MIT/public-domain) are redistributed with their exact notices in the SBOM. The MSVC compiler and static runtime are covered by the GPL-2.0 section 3 system-component exception and Microsoft's redistributable-runtime terms; the SBOM records the exact versions used.

components.description: Repository documentation, automation, tests, and tools outside the separately enumerated components; all project-authored GPL material.
components.holder: ZaeREo contributors and referenced upstream GPL authors
components.jurisdiction_scope: All public repository, history, source-archive, CI, and release channels.
components.revisit_condition: Revisit if repository authorship or licensing changes.
components.notes: Repository documentation, automation, tests, and tools authored for the project and distributed under the GPL.

required_predicates: Every artifact member has an exact permitted file decision and immutable release-manifest hash.
Every source component and generated byte has an applicable outbound license and evidence record.
The complete repository tree and reachable history are the separately reviewed history-clean tools root.
The tools-progress readiness profile passes deterministic, security, SBOM, checksum, and non-playable-label gates.
prohibited_content: DLLs, executables, object files, symbols, compiler output, or dependency binaries
Gameplay or Zaero-derived source, tests, audits, definitions, or references outside the exact file list
Original or imported Zaero media and any local-full output
Files reached only through a broad glob, directory rule, or unreviewed generated archive member
unmet_requirements: The optional history-clean tools distribution root has not been created.
No tools-only package manifest, SBOM, checksum set, or tools-progress readiness record exists yet.
modes.notes: A narrow, non-playable artifact of the GPL automation. Optional; the primary end-user mode is asset-full.

required_predicates: The GPL DLL ships with complete corresponding source.
The package omits bundled media; completion rebuilds the pack from a user's existing Zaero installation.
The exact package, notices, SBOM, checksums, source bundle, and playable readiness record pass, then a maintainer approves the draft.
prohibited_content: Bundled Zaero media (this mode rebuilds it locally; use asset-full to bundle)
unmet_requirements: The importer-kit file allowlist and corresponding-source bundle have not been assembled.
Live map/save validation and a playable-candidate readiness record are pending.
modes.notes: Ships the DLL and rebuilds the content pack from a user's existing installation, for users who prefer that over the bundled asset-full package.

required_predicates: The GPL DLL and ported GPL content are bundled together with notices and credits.
The exact package, editor gamepack, notices, SBOM, checksums, and corresponding source pass, then a maintainer approves the draft release.
prohibited_content: The Rerelease soundtrack or base game data (not GPL Zaero content)
unmet_requirements: The asset-full file allowlist and shipped-content manifest have not been finalized.
Live map/save validation and a playable-candidate readiness record are pending.
modes.notes: The primary end-user mode: bundles the ported Zaero content directly, the way the sibling REBLIVION port ships Oblivion.

required_predicates: The build is assembled locally from the tracked/bundled content or a user's installation.
Output stays under ignored local roots as unvalidated developer scratch.
prohibited_content: Any public upload of this unvalidated developer scratch
modes.notes: Developer-only build used during validation; kept out of release channels because it is unvalidated scratch, not for any rights reason.

non_waivable_rules.description: The distribution mode and every repository, CI, source, editor, and release channel must be consistent and carry the GPL notices before a release is cut.
A local-full artifact is unvalidated developer scratch and is never published or promoted into a release mode.
The candidate must be built from the recorded clean source commit and allowed distribution root with no dirty or unreviewed bytes.
Tag, VERSION, package metadata, manifests, source, binary, and release notes must identify the same exact release.
Every archive checksum and member must match the exact reviewed per-file allowlist and internal/external manifests.
Required critical security, path-safety, archive, importer, installer, and publisher checks must pass for the exact candidate.
Every shipped byte must have its applicable license and notices; a DLL must have complete corresponding source for the exact binary without implying missing grants.
Every profile-required feature, entity, quirk, map, mode, save, multiplayer, editor, package, and live evidence gate must pass without stale or missing proof.

waiver_policy.notes: A reviewed decision may waive only an explicitly optional or deferred gate and remains visible in readiness output. It can never change a false playable-stable result to true.

notes: ZaeREo redistributes the GPL Quake II Rerelease DLL and the GPL Zaero source and assets under the GPL. Code and media distribution are permitted; a public release is gated only on engineering readiness plus human approval of a draft release. local-full stays private as unvalidated developer scratch.
